unicode.value: DIPLOMA Foundations of ECDE and Curriculum Development

The Kenya National Examinations Council
Diploma in Early Childhood Development and Education
December 2008

Instructions to Candidates:

This paper consists of SIX questions. Answer any FIVE questions in the answer booklet provided. Each question carries a total of 20 marks.

Question 1

(a) State four effects of the recommendations of the Gachathi Commission on the Development of Early Childhood Development and Education in Kenya.

Sample Answer:

  • Increased funding for ECDE programs.
  • Expansion of ECDE centers across the country.
  • Improved teacher training and certification.
  • Integration of ECDE into the national education system.

Question 2

(b) Explain nine roles of the community in the implementation of Early Childhood Development and Education programme, according to the Early Childhood Development and Education policy framework.

Sample Answer:

  1. Providing a conducive learning environment.
  2. Participating in the management of ECDE centers.
  3. Supporting the construction and maintenance of facilities.
  4. Encouraging parental involvement in children's education.
  5. Contributing to the development of local curricula.
  6. Providing cultural and traditional knowledge.
  7. Ensuring child protection and welfare.
  8. Organizing community events that promote education.
  9. Monitoring and evaluating the ECDE program's effectiveness.

Question 3

(c) State three ways in which each of the following emerging issues affect child growth and development: (i) urbanization; (ii) changing family structure.

Sample Answer:

(i) Urbanization:

  • Increased exposure to diverse cultures and technologies.
  • Potential for higher stress due to crowded living conditions.
  • Access to better educational resources and healthcare.

(ii) Changing family structure:

  • Impact of single-parent households on child emotional development.
  • Increased reliance on extended family or caregivers.
  • Effects of blended families on social relationships.

Question 4

(a) Describe the four stages of Ralph Tyler’s theory of the curriculum development process.

Sample Answer:

  1. Determining educational objectives.
  2. Selecting learning experiences.
  3. Organizing learning experiences.
  4. Evaluating the curriculum's effectiveness.

Question 5

(c) Explain five ways you would use to develop social equality when handling children in the Early Childhood Development and Education curriculum.

Sample Answer:

  • Ensuring inclusive educational activities that accommodate all children.
  • Providing equal access to resources and learning materials.
  • Encouraging interactions among children from diverse backgrounds.
  • Implementing anti-bias education to promote equality.
  • Training teachers to recognize and address social inequalities.

Question 6

(b) Give five reasons why the stage of evaluation in curriculum development is important.

Sample Answer:

  1. To assess whether educational objectives are being met.
  2. To identify areas that need improvement.
  3. To ensure the curriculum remains relevant and effective.
  4. To provide feedback for curriculum revision.
  5. To measure student learning outcomes and progress.

© 2008 The Kenya National Examinations Council

Diploma in Early Childhood Development and Education
Foundation of ECDE and Curriculum Development - December 2014

Instructions to Candidates:

  • Write your name and index number in the spaces provided above.
  • Sign and write the date of the examination in the spaces provided above.
  • This question paper consists of SIX questions.
  • Answer any FIVE questions in the spaces provided after question 6.
  • Each question carries a total of 20 marks.
  • Do not remove any pages from this booklet.
  • Candidates should answer the questions in English.

Question 1

(a) Outline six benefits of children to parents in the Traditional African context. (6 marks)

  • Children provide labor for family chores and agricultural activities.
  • They ensure the continuation of the family lineage.
  • Children offer social security to parents in old age.
  • They enhance the social status of parents within the community.
  • Children contribute to the family's economic well-being through their future earnings.
  • They bring joy and emotional fulfillment to parents.

(b) Examine six negative effects of HIV and AIDS on the ECDE teacher. (6 marks)

  • Increased absenteeism due to illness.
  • Reduced productivity and performance.
  • Emotional and psychological stress.
  • Financial strain due to medical expenses.
  • Stigmatization and discrimination from colleagues and the community.
  • Increased workload for healthy teachers due to staff shortages.

(c) Explain four benefits of a sociology lesson to an ECDE teacher. (8 marks)

  • Enhances understanding of social dynamics and cultural diversity.
  • Improves ability to address social issues affecting children.
  • Promotes effective communication with parents and the community.
  • Encourages the development of inclusive teaching practices.

Question 2

(a) Identify four objectives of ECDE in Uganda. (4 marks)

  • To promote holistic development of children.
  • To prepare children for primary education.
  • To enhance children's social and emotional skills.
  • To provide a foundation for lifelong learning.

(b) Discuss four contributions of Maria Montessori in the development of ECDE curriculum. (8 marks)

  • Emphasized child-centered learning.
  • Introduced the use of sensory-based learning materials.
  • Advocated for individualized learning plans.
  • Promoted the importance of a prepared learning environment.

(c) Suggest four measures the Government of Kenya should put in place to ensure provision of quality education in early childhood in Arid and Semi-arid Lands. (8 marks)

  • Increase funding for ECDE programs in these regions.
  • Provide training and support for ECDE teachers.
  • Improve infrastructure and learning materials.
  • Implement community awareness programs on the importance of early childhood education.

Question 3

(a) Identify four forms of child abuse that can take place in an ECDE centre. (4 marks)

  • Physical abuse.
  • Emotional abuse.
  • Sexual abuse.
  • Neglect.

(b) Describe four characteristics of Traditional African Education. (8 marks)

  • It was community-oriented and focused on communal values.
  • It emphasized practical skills and vocational training.
  • It was oral-based, with knowledge passed down through storytelling.
  • It included initiation rites as a form of transition to adulthood.

(c) Explain four ways in which an ECDE teacher can use language activities to promote hard work in the learners. (8 marks)

  • Encourage storytelling that highlights the value of hard work.
  • Use songs and rhymes that promote diligence and perseverance.
  • Incorporate role-playing activities that model hard work.
  • Provide positive reinforcement and praise for effort and achievement.

Question 4

(a) Differentiate between curriculum and syllabus. (4 marks)

  • Curriculum refers to the overall content, structure, and objectives of an educational program.
  • Syllabus is a detailed outline of topics to be covered in a specific course or subject.

(b) Describe three elements of school curriculum according to Gilbert P. Oltoch. (6 marks)

  • Objectives: The goals and outcomes the curriculum aims to achieve.
  • Content: The subject matter and topics included in the curriculum.
  • Evaluation: The methods used to assess student learning and curriculum effectiveness.

(c) Explain five functions of NACECE in curriculum development. (10 marks)

  • Developing and reviewing ECDE curriculum.
  • Providing training and support for ECDE teachers.
  • Conducting research on early childhood education.
  • Monitoring and evaluating the implementation of ECDE programs.
  • Advising the government on ECDE policies and practices.

Question 5

(a) Identify four categories of children who may not easily access regular ECDE services. (4 marks)

  • Children with disabilities.
  • Children from marginalized communities.
  • Orphans and vulnerable children.
  • Children in remote or conflict-affected areas.

(b) Highlight three negative effects of liberalization of ECDE Teacher training in the country. (6 marks)

  • Decline in the quality of teacher training programs.
  • Increased commercialization of teacher training.
  • Lack of standardization and regulation in training institutions.

(c) Explain five achievements of sessional paper No.1 of 2005 in the ECDE sector. (10 marks)

  • Increased enrollment in ECDE programs.
  • Improved infrastructure and learning materials.
  • Enhanced teacher training and professional development.
  • Strengthened policy framework for early childhood education.
  • Increased community involvement in ECDE programs.

Question 6

(a) Explain the roles of the following in curriculum development process. (i) course panel (3 marks) (ii) academic board (3 marks)

  • Course Panel: Responsible for designing and reviewing the curriculum, ensuring it meets educational standards and objectives.
  • Academic Board: Oversees the overall academic policies and approves the curriculum developed by the course panel.

(b) Outline six roles of the Directorate of Quality Assurance and Standards in the implementation of ECDE curriculum in Kenya. (6 marks)

  • Monitoring and evaluating the implementation of the curriculum.
  • Providing guidelines and standards for curriculum delivery.
  • Conducting inspections and assessments of ECDE centers.
  • Offering training and support to ECDE teachers.
  • Ensuring compliance with national education policies.
  • Collecting and analyzing data on curriculum effectiveness.

(c) Describe four merits of evaluation in ECDE curriculum. (8 marks)

  • Identifies strengths and weaknesses in the curriculum.
  • Provides feedback for continuous improvement.
  • Ensures accountability and quality assurance.
  • Supports decision-making for curriculum adjustments.

© 2014 The Kenya National Examinations Council

Foundations of ECDE and Curriculum Development - December 2015

Instructions to Candidates:

1. Write your name and index number in the spaces provided above.

2. Sign and write the date of examination in the spaces provided above.

3. This paper consists of SIX questions.

4. Answer any FIVE questions in the spaces provided after question 6.

5. Each question carries a total of 20 marks.

6. Do NOT remove any pages from this booklet.

7. Candidates should answer the questions in English.

Question Maximum Score Candidate’s Score
1 20
2 20
3 20
4 20
5 20
6 20
TOTAL SCORE 100

Question 1

(a) Outline two positive effects of the Gachathi Report (1976) to ECDE. (4 marks)

1. The Gachathi Report led to the establishment of more Early Childhood Development and Education (ECDE) centers, increasing access to early education.

2. It emphasized the importance of teacher training, leading to improved quality of education in ECDE centers.

(b) Explain three factors of urbanization that have impacted positively on Early Childhood Development and Education. (6 marks)

1. Urbanization has led to better infrastructure, such as schools and healthcare facilities, which support ECDE.

2. Increased access to information and communication technologies (ICT) in urban areas has enhanced learning opportunities for young children.

3. Urban areas often have more diverse populations, which can enrich the cultural experiences of children in ECDE centers.

(c) “Education in Kenya should foster nationalism, patriotism and promote national unity”. State five learning activities through which the above national goal of education can be implemented in ECDE. (10 marks)

1. Celebrating national holidays and teaching children about their significance.

2. Singing the national anthem and other patriotic songs.

3. Organizing cultural days where children learn about different ethnic groups in Kenya.

4. Teaching children about national heroes and their contributions to the country.

5. Encouraging community service projects that promote unity and cooperation.

Question 2

(a) State two ways in which home-based care centres have enhanced early childhood learning. (4 marks)

1. Home-based care centers provide a familiar and comfortable environment for young children, which can enhance their learning experience.

2. They offer personalized attention to children, allowing for tailored learning experiences that meet individual needs.

(b) Highlight three positive skills acquired by children from watching television. (6 marks)

1. Language and communication skills through educational programs and storytelling.

2. Cognitive skills such as problem-solving and critical thinking from interactive shows.

3. Social skills by observing and learning from characters' interactions and behaviors.

(c) Identify five protective rights as indicated in the Children’s Act, 2001. (10 marks)

1. The right to protection from abuse, neglect, and exploitation.

2. The right to education and access to educational materials.

3. The right to healthcare and access to medical services.

4. The right to protection from harmful cultural practices.

5. The right to be protected from child labor and hazardous work.

Question 3

(a) Highlight two challenges experienced by ECDE teachers working with pastoral communities. (4 marks)

1. Limited access to educational resources and materials due to the remote locations of pastoral communities.

2. High mobility of pastoral families, which can lead to inconsistent attendance of children in ECDE centers.

(b) Outline four characteristics of ECDE centres established by the missionaries at pre-independence. (8 marks)

1. They were often attached to churches and had a strong religious influence on the curriculum.

2. The centers focused on basic literacy and numeracy skills.

3. They provided education primarily to the children of converts and local elites.

4. The centers were often underfunded and relied on donations and support from missionary organizations.

(c) “The learners were busy and enjoying the free-choice activities; The teacher sung a familiar song for them to stop and change over”. Highlight four advantages of involving learners in the clearing up activity. (8 marks)

1. It teaches children responsibility and the importance of taking care of their environment.

2. It helps develop fine motor skills as children handle and organize materials.

3. It encourages teamwork and cooperation among children.

4. It provides a smooth transition between activities, reducing chaos and confusion.

Question 4

(a) State the meaning of the term education. (2 marks)

Education is the process of facilitating learning, acquiring knowledge, skills, values, beliefs, and habits through various methods such as teaching, training, or research.

(b) Explain the sources of curriculum objectives according to Ralph Tyler. (6 marks)

1. Studies of the learners themselves: Understanding the needs, interests, and abilities of the students.

2. Studies of contemporary life: Identifying the skills and knowledge needed in modern society.

3. Suggestions from subject matter specialists: Input from experts in various fields to determine essential content.

(c) Using an illustration, explain Hilda Taba’s model of curriculum development. (12 marks)

Hilda Taba’s model of curriculum development is a grassroots approach that involves teachers in the process. The model consists of the following steps:

  1. Diagnosis of needs: Identifying the needs of the learners and the community.
  2. Formulation of objectives: Defining clear and specific learning objectives based on the diagnosed needs.
  3. Selection of content: Choosing appropriate content that aligns with the objectives.
  4. Organization of content: Structuring the content in a logical and meaningful sequence.
  5. Selection of learning experiences: Designing activities and experiences that facilitate learning.
  6. Organization of learning experiences: Arranging the learning experiences in a coherent manner.
  7. Evaluation: Assessing the effectiveness of the curriculum and making necessary adjustments.

This model emphasizes the importance of teacher involvement and a bottom-up approach to curriculum development.

Question 5

(a) State two roles of District Centres for Early Childhood Education in the implementation of Early Childhood Development and Education curriculum. (2 marks)

1. Providing training and professional development for ECDE teachers.

2. Monitoring and evaluating the implementation of the ECDE curriculum in local centers.

(b) Describe four ways of imparting social skills to ECDE learners. (8 marks)

1. Encouraging group activities and cooperative play to foster teamwork and communication.

2. Teaching children to share and take turns through structured games and activities.

3. Modeling positive social behaviors and interactions for children to emulate.

4. Using role-playing and storytelling to teach empathy and understanding of others' feelings.

(c) Explain five activities each from the sub theme: Sun, that a preschool teacher can organise for five-year old’s, in Creative arts and Science lessons. (10 marks)

Creative Arts:

  1. Drawing and painting pictures of the sun.
  2. Creating sun-shaped crafts using paper plates and yellow paint.
  3. Singing songs about the sun and its importance.
  4. Making sun catchers using colored tissue paper and clear contact paper.
  5. Role-playing as the sun and other celestial bodies in a simple play.

Science:

  1. Observing and discussing the effects of sunlight on plants.
  2. Conducting simple experiments to show how the sun affects temperature.
  3. Creating a sundial to understand how the sun's position changes throughout the day.
  4. Discussing the importance of the sun for life on Earth.
  5. Exploring the concept of day and night using a globe and a flashlight to represent the sun.

Question 6

(a) Highlight two measures an ECDE teacher may use to discourage early marriages. (4 marks)

1. Educating children and their parents about the negative consequences of early marriage, such as health risks and limited educational opportunities.

2. Promoting gender equality and empowering girls through education and awareness programs.

(b) Outline three measures that can be undertaken to improve health, safety and nutrition within ECDE centres. (6 marks)

1. Implementing regular health check-ups and vaccinations for children.

2. Ensuring that the ECDE centers have safe and clean environments, including proper sanitation and hygiene practices.

3. Providing balanced and nutritious meals to children, and educating parents about the importance of good nutrition.

(c) (i) State two challenges the government may face in implementing ICT in ECDE. (4 marks)

1. Limited infrastructure and access to technology in rural areas.

2. Lack of trained teachers who can effectively integrate ICT into the curriculum.

(ii) Identify three ways in which ICT can enhance the learning environment in Early Childhood Development Education Centres. (6 marks)

1. Providing interactive and engaging learning materials, such as educational games and videos.

2. Facilitating communication and collaboration between teachers, parents, and students through digital platforms.

3. Offering access to a wide range of online resources and information that can supplement traditional teaching methods.

© 2015 The Kenya National Examinations Council

Kenya National Examinations Council

Diploma in Early Childhood Development and Education

Foundations of ECDE and Curriculum Development

November 2016 - Time: 3 hours

Instructions to Candidates

  • Write your name and index number in the spaces provided above.
  • Sign and write the date of examination in the spaces provided above.
  • This paper consists of SIX questions.
  • Answer any FIVE questions in the spaces provided after question 6.
  • Each question carries a total of 20 marks.
  • Do NOT remove any pages from this booklet.
  • Candidates should answer the questions in English.

Questions and Sample Answers

  1. (a) Distinguish between the philosophies of Johann H. Pestalozzi and Friedrich W. Froebel in Early Childhood Development and Education. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    • Johann H. Pestalozzi: Emphasized the importance of hands-on learning and the role of the teacher as a guide. He believed in educating the "head, heart, and hands" to develop a child holistically.
    • Friedrich W. Froebel: Known as the father of the kindergarten system, Froebel focused on play-based learning and the use of toys (gifts) to stimulate creativity and imagination in children.

    (b) Explain the following provisions as outlined in the Children’s Act, 2001:

    (i) Right to parental care; (3 marks)

    Answer:

    The Children’s Act, 2001, guarantees every child the right to parental care, which includes the provision of basic needs such as food, shelter, education, and emotional support. Parents or guardians are legally obligated to ensure the well-being and development of their children.

    (ii) Protection from child labour and armed conflict. (3 marks)

    Answer:

    The Act prohibits the engagement of children in labor that is harmful to their health, education, or development. It also protects children from being recruited or used in armed conflicts, ensuring their safety and well-being.

    (c) Discuss five reasons that make philosophy relevant to ECDE teachers. (10 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Helps teachers understand the purpose and goals of education.
    2. Guides teachers in developing child-centered teaching methods.
    3. Encourages critical thinking and reflection on teaching practices.
    4. Promotes the holistic development of children (physical, emotional, social, and cognitive).
    5. Supports the creation of inclusive and equitable learning environments.

  2. (a) State two differences between The African Charter on the Rights and Welfare of the Child, 1990 and the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child, 1989. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. African Charter: Focuses on the specific cultural and social context of African children. UN Convention: Provides a universal framework applicable to all children globally.
    2. African Charter: Emphasizes the role of the extended family and community in child-rearing. UN Convention: Focuses more on the rights of the child within the nuclear family.

    (b) Explain four positive ways in which children were perceived in African Traditional Societies. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Children were seen as a blessing and a sign of prosperity.
    2. They were valued as future leaders and contributors to the community.
    3. Children were considered a link between the past (ancestors) and the future.
    4. They were seen as a source of joy and pride for the family and community.

    (c) Outline four negative effects of illiterate parents on ECDE learners. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Limited ability to support children’s learning at home.
    2. Difficulty in understanding the importance of early childhood education.
    3. Inability to assist with homework or school-related activities.
    4. Lower likelihood of advocating for their children’s educational needs.

  3. (a) Identify two similarities of ECDE objectives in Kenya and Ghana. (2 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Both aim to promote holistic development (physical, emotional, social, and cognitive).
    2. Both emphasize the importance of preparing children for primary education.

    (b) State four achievements of the pre-school education project co-founded by the Ministry of Education, Science and Technology and Bernard Van Leer Foundation. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Improved access to pre-school education in underserved areas.
    2. Enhanced teacher training and professional development.
    3. Development of child-centered teaching materials and resources.
    4. Increased community involvement in early childhood education.

    (c) Explain five ways in which ECDE learners may benefit from mobile schools as a measure to increase access to basic education. (10 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Mobile schools bring education to remote and marginalized areas.
    2. They provide flexible learning schedules for children who cannot attend regular schools.
    3. Mobile schools offer tailored curricula to meet the needs of specific communities.
    4. They promote inclusivity by reaching out-of-school children.
    5. Mobile schools often provide additional support services, such as meals and healthcare.

  4. (a) Explain the term syllabus. (2 marks)

    Answer:

    A syllabus is a document that outlines the topics, content, and learning objectives for a specific subject or course. It serves as a guide for teachers and students in the teaching and learning process.

    (b) State four approaches of sustaining learners' attention in an ECDE class. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Use of interactive and hands-on activities.
    2. Incorporating games and play into lessons.
    3. Using visual aids and multimedia resources.
    4. Maintaining a positive and engaging classroom environment.

    (c) Identify five factors considered when including topics of a subject in the syllabus. (10 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Relevance to the learners' age and developmental stage.
    2. Alignment with the overall goals of the curriculum.
    3. Availability of teaching resources and materials.
    4. Cultural and social relevance to the learners' context.
    5. Potential to promote critical thinking and problem-solving skills.

  5. (a) Give two benefits of devolving ECDE services to the county governments. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Improved access to ECDE services at the local level.
    2. Enhanced community involvement and ownership of ECDE programs.

    (b) Describe three challenges the Kindergarten Headmistresses’ Association (KHA) may face in performing its duties. (6 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Limited funding and resources to support ECDE programs.
    2. Difficulty in coordinating activities across different regions.
    3. Lack of adequate training and professional development for members.

    (c) Explain five functions of Education Standard and Quality Assurance Council in ECDE curriculum development. (10 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Setting and maintaining standards for ECDE curriculum and teaching practices.
    2. Monitoring and evaluating the implementation of the curriculum.
    3. Providing guidelines for teacher training and professional development.
    4. Ensuring the quality of teaching and learning materials.
    5. Conducting research to inform curriculum improvements.

  6. (a) Identify four ways in which national unity can be nurtured in an ECDE centre. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Promoting cultural diversity and inclusivity in the classroom.
    2. Teaching children about the importance of national unity and cohesion.
    3. Encouraging teamwork and collaboration among learners.
    4. Celebrating national holidays and cultural events.

    (b) Name and explain the three levels of presentation that children go through according to Jerome Brunner. (6 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Enactive Level: Children learn through physical actions and hands-on experiences.
    2. Iconic Level: Children use visual representations, such as pictures and diagrams, to understand concepts.
    3. Symbolic Level: Children use abstract symbols, such as language and numbers, to represent ideas.

    (c) Describe five contributions of Jean Piaget to Early Childhood Development and Education. (10 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Piaget’s theory of cognitive development emphasizes the importance of stages in children’s learning.
    2. He highlighted the role of play in cognitive development.
    3. Piaget’s work influenced the development of child-centered learning approaches.
    4. He emphasized the importance of hands-on and experiential learning.
    5. Piaget’s research provided a foundation for understanding how children construct knowledge.

This paper consists of 16 printed pages.

Candidates should check the question paper to ascertain that all the pages are printed as indicated and that no questions are missing.

© 2016 The Kenya National Examinations Council.

Kenya National Examinations Council

Diploma in Early Childhood Development and Education

Foundations of ECDE and Curriculum Development

November 2017 - Time: 3 hours

Instructions to Candidates

  • Write your name and index number in the spaces provided above.
  • Sign and write the date of examination in the spaces provided above.
  • This paper consists of SIX questions.
  • Answer any FIVE questions in the spaces provided after question 6.
  • Each question carries a total of 20 marks.
  • Do NOT remove any pages from this booklet.
  • Candidates should answer the questions in English.

Questions and Sample Answers

  1. (a) (i) State two characteristics of Early Childhood Development and Education. (2 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Focuses on holistic development (physical, emotional, social, and cognitive).
    2. Emphasizes play-based learning to foster creativity and curiosity.

    (a) (ii) Outline two practices adopted from Maria Montessori in Early Childhood Development and Education. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Use of child-centered learning materials that encourage self-directed learning.
    2. Emphasis on hands-on activities and sensory-based learning.

    (b) Highlight three differences between the traditional and contemporary concept of the African child. (6 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Traditional: The child is seen as a communal responsibility. Contemporary: The child is primarily the responsibility of the nuclear family.
    2. Traditional: Education is informal and based on cultural practices. Contemporary: Education is formal and structured.
    3. Traditional: Children are expected to conform to societal norms. Contemporary: Children are encouraged to express individuality.

    (c) Explain four challenges of integrating pre-primary into the primary school system. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Lack of adequate infrastructure for young children in primary schools.
    2. Insufficient training for primary school teachers to handle pre-primary learners.
    3. Mismatch between the developmental needs of pre-primary children and the primary school curriculum.
    4. Limited resources for play-based learning in primary schools.

  2. (a) State four ways in which needs assessment is important in the curriculum development process. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Identifies the specific needs of learners.
    2. Helps in setting clear and achievable curriculum objectives.
    3. Ensures the curriculum is relevant to the learners' context.
    4. Guides the allocation of resources effectively.

    (b) Describe three contributions of Jean Piaget to ECDE. (6 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Piaget’s theory of cognitive development emphasizes the importance of stages in children’s learning.
    2. He highlighted the role of play in cognitive development.
    3. Piaget’s work influenced the development of child-centered learning approaches.

    (c) (i) State two main activities a teacher would use to instil obedience in ECDE learners. (2 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Setting clear rules and expectations for behavior.
    2. Using positive reinforcement to reward good behavior.

    (c) (ii) Identify four reasons for considering chronological age of a child before admission to pre-school. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. To ensure the child is developmentally ready for structured learning.
    2. To align the child’s cognitive abilities with the curriculum.
    3. To promote social interaction with peers of similar age.
    4. To avoid overwhelming the child with tasks beyond their developmental stage.

  3. (a) Explain the term subject as used in curriculum development. (2 marks)

    Answer:

    A subject refers to a specific area of knowledge or study within the curriculum, such as mathematics, science, or language arts.

    (b) (i) State four features of a day care centre. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Provides care and supervision for young children.
    2. Offers structured activities to promote learning and development.
    3. Has trained caregivers or teachers.
    4. Includes facilities for play, rest, and meals.

    (b) (ii) Highlight three possible challenges the county governments may face in managing day care centres. (6 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Lack of adequate funding to maintain and improve facilities.
    2. Insufficient trained staff to meet the needs of children.
    3. Difficulty in ensuring consistent quality across different centres.

    (c) Describe four activities that can enhance the development of reading skills in preschoolers. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Reading aloud to children to build vocabulary and comprehension.
    2. Using picture books to encourage storytelling and imagination.
    3. Introducing phonics games to develop letter-sound recognition.
    4. Encouraging children to retell stories in their own words.

  4. (a) Identify four effects of poverty on the development of ECDE infrastructure. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Lack of funds to build and maintain ECDE facilities.
    2. Inadequate learning materials and resources.
    3. Poor sanitation and hygiene facilities.
    4. Overcrowded classrooms due to limited infrastructure.

    (b) Describe four economic factors that lead to curriculum change. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Changes in labor market demands requiring new skills.
    2. Economic growth leading to increased investment in education.
    3. Globalization influencing the need for a more competitive workforce.
    4. Government policies aimed at reducing unemployment through education.

    (c) Discuss four negative socio-economic effects of single parenthood on an ECDE learner. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Financial strain leading to limited access to educational resources.
    2. Emotional stress affecting the child’s ability to concentrate in school.
    3. Lack of parental supervision due to work commitments.
    4. Social stigma affecting the child’s self-esteem and social interactions.

  5. (a) Identify four differences in the functions of DICECE and NACECE. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. DICECE: Focuses on implementing ECDE programs at the district level. NACECE: Provides national policy guidance and coordination.
    2. DICECE: Conducts training for ECDE teachers at the local level. NACECE: Develops national training curricula and standards.
    3. DICECE: Monitors and evaluates ECDE programs within the district. NACECE: Conducts research and evaluation at the national level.
    4. DICECE: Works closely with local communities. NACECE: Collaborates with international organizations and stakeholders.

    (b) (i) State four roles of the university in ECDE curriculum development. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Conducting research to inform curriculum development.
    2. Training ECDE professionals and curriculum developers.
    3. Providing expertise in curriculum design and evaluation.
    4. Collaborating with government and NGOs to implement curriculum changes.

    (b) (ii) Discuss two effects of breakdown of extended families on a child's upbringing. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Loss of communal support in child-rearing, leading to increased pressure on nuclear families.
    2. Reduced exposure to cultural values and traditions traditionally passed down by extended family members.

    (c) Explain four measures an ECDE teacher can adopt to enhance participatory rights of a learner. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Encouraging children to express their opinions and ideas in class.
    2. Involving learners in decision-making processes, such as choosing classroom activities.
    3. Creating a safe and inclusive environment where all children feel valued.
    4. Teaching children about their rights and responsibilities in a child-friendly manner.

  6. (a) Highlight three negative effects of African resistance movement to colonial rule on ECDE development in Kenya. (6 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Disruption of traditional education systems and cultural practices.
    2. Destruction of infrastructure, including schools, during conflicts.
    3. Loss of skilled educators who were involved in the resistance movement.

    (b) Explain three measures an ECDE teacher can put in place to curb bullying amongst the learners. (6 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Establishing clear anti-bullying policies and consequences.
    2. Teaching children about empathy and respect for others.
    3. Encouraging open communication and reporting of bullying incidents.

    (c) Outline four practices an ECDE teacher can inculcate in the learners to enhance environmental hygiene. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Teaching children to wash their hands regularly.
    2. Encouraging proper waste disposal and recycling.
    3. Promoting cleanliness in the classroom and playground.
    4. Educating children about the importance of clean water and sanitation.

This paper consists of 16 printed pages.

Candidates should check the question paper to ascertain that all the pages are printed as indicated and that no questions are missing.

© 2017 The Kenya National Examinations Council.

Kenya National Examinations Council

Diploma in Early Childhood Development and Education

Foundations of ECDE and Curriculum Development

April 2019 - Time: 3 hours

Instructions to Candidates

  • Write your name and index number in the spaces provided above.
  • Sign and write the date of examination in the spaces provided above.
  • This question paper consists of SIX questions.
  • Answer any FIVE questions in the spaces provided after question 6.
  • Each question carries a total of 20 marks.
  • Do NOT remove any pages from this booklet.
  • Candidates should answer the questions in English.

Questions and Sample Answers

  1. (a) Describe two differences between Philosophy and Sociology in Early Childhood Development and Education. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    • Philosophy: Focuses on the purpose and meaning of education, while Sociology examines how society and culture influence education.
    • Philosophy: Deals with abstract ideas like morality and ethics, whereas Sociology studies social structures and their impact on learning.

    (b) Explain three curriculum objectives of Early Childhood Development and Education. (6 marks)

    Answer:

    1. To promote holistic development (physical, emotional, social, and cognitive).
    2. To prepare children for primary education by developing foundational skills.
    3. To foster a love for learning and curiosity in children.

    (c) In the African Traditional Society, the child does not belong to the family. Discuss five communal responsibilities in child rearing. (10 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Shared Caregiving: Extended family members help raise the child.
    2. Moral Education: The community teaches values and cultural norms.
    3. Discipline: Elders correct and guide the child’s behavior.
    4. Economic Support: The community contributes resources for the child’s upbringing.
    5. Socialization: The child learns social skills through interaction with the community.

  2. (a) Highlight four perceptions of pregnancy in African Traditional Society. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Pregnancy is seen as a blessing and a sign of fertility.
    2. It is viewed as a communal event, with the entire community celebrating and supporting the expectant mother.
    3. Certain taboos and rituals are observed to protect the mother and unborn child.
    4. Pregnancy is often associated with spiritual significance, such as ancestral blessings.

    (b) Explain four negative effects of Information Communication Technology (ICT) on ECDE learners. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Reduced Physical Activity: Excessive screen time can lead to sedentary lifestyles.
    2. Social Isolation: Overuse of ICT can reduce face-to-face interactions.
    3. Distraction: ICT devices can divert attention from learning activities.
    4. Exposure to Inappropriate Content: Young children may access unsuitable material online.

    (c) Discuss four ways in which an ECDE teacher may adopt to promote social equity in class. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Use inclusive teaching materials that reflect diverse cultures and backgrounds.
    2. Encourage group activities to foster collaboration and understanding among learners.
    3. Address biases and stereotypes in the classroom.
    4. Provide equal opportunities for all learners to participate and succeed.

  3. (a) Identify two similarities and two differences between Kenya and Uganda ECDE organizational structures. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    • Similarities:
      1. Both countries have government bodies overseeing ECDE (e.g., Ministry of Education).
      2. Both emphasize community involvement in ECDE programs.
    • Differences:
      1. Kenya has a more centralized ECDE system, while Uganda’s system is more decentralized.
      2. Kenya’s ECDE curriculum is more standardized compared to Uganda’s.

    (b) Discuss four roles of the Teachers Service Commission in implementing ECDE curriculum. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Recruiting and deploying qualified ECDE teachers.
    2. Providing professional development and training for teachers.
    3. Ensuring teachers adhere to ethical standards and curriculum guidelines.
    4. Monitoring and evaluating teacher performance.

    (c) Explain four negative effects of gender imbalance in the ECDE teaching profession. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Lack of male role models for young boys.
    2. Reinforcement of gender stereotypes (e.g., teaching as a “female” profession).
    3. Limited diversity in teaching approaches and perspectives.
    4. Potential for gender bias in classroom management.

  4. (a) State four reasons why a teacher trainee should study history of ECDE. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. To understand the evolution of ECDE policies and practices.
    2. To learn from past successes and failures in ECDE.
    3. To appreciate the cultural and social context of early childhood education.
    4. To inform current and future ECDE curriculum development.

    (b) Discuss four factors which may influence ECDE learners into drug and substance abuse. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Peer pressure from older children or adults.
    2. Exposure to drug use in the family or community.
    3. Lack of parental supervision and guidance.
    4. Stress or trauma from challenging home environments.

    (c) Highlight four ways in which evaluation is important in Early Childhood Development and Education curriculum. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. To assess the effectiveness of teaching methods and materials.
    2. To identify areas where learners need additional support.
    3. To ensure the curriculum meets its objectives.
    4. To provide feedback for continuous improvement of the curriculum.

  5. (a) Explain the term curriculum as used in Early Childhood Development and Education. (2 marks)

    Answer:

    The curriculum refers to the planned learning experiences and activities designed to achieve specific educational goals for young children.

    (b) Describe four factors that an ECDE teacher should consider when developing curriculum support materials. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Age-appropriateness of the materials.
    2. Cultural relevance and inclusivity.
    3. Availability of resources.
    4. Alignment with curriculum objectives.

    (c) Discuss five ways a teacher can enhance creativity skills to an ECDE learner. (10 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Provide open-ended activities (e.g., drawing, storytelling).
    2. Encourage exploration and experimentation.
    3. Use play-based learning to stimulate imagination.
    4. Praise and reward creative efforts.
    5. Integrate arts and crafts into daily activities.

  6. (a) Highlight two dimensions of G. P. Oltoch’s model of school curriculum. (4 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Content: What is taught (knowledge, skills, values).
    2. Process: How it is taught (teaching methods, interactions).

    (b) Outline four ways an ECDE teacher can promote individual development and self-fulfillment among learners. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Provide personalized learning experiences.
    2. Encourage self-expression through art, music, and drama.
    3. Set achievable goals to build confidence.
    4. Foster a supportive and inclusive classroom environment.

    (c) Describe four ways in which insecurity affects ECDE development in Kenya. (8 marks)

    Answer:

    1. Disruption of learning due to conflicts or violence.
    2. Limited access to ECDE centers in insecure areas.
    3. Trauma and emotional stress affecting children’s development.
    4. Reduced funding for ECDE programs in favor of security measures.

This paper consists of 16 printed pages.

Candidates should check the question paper to ascertain that all the pages are printed as indicated and that no questions are missing.

© 2019 The Kenya National Examinations Council.
